description: This figure presents a transmission electron microscopy (TEM) bright-field image showing a NbC precipitate within the austenite (γ) matrix of Fe-12Mn-9Al-3Cr-1.4C-0.02Nb low-density steel. The main micrograph displays a dark, submicron particle labeled "Spot 1" embedded in the γ matrix, with a scale bar indicating 500 nm. The inset shows a selected area electron diffraction (SAED) pattern acquired from Spot 1, revealing the crystallographic orientation relationship [100]$_\gamma$ // [100]$_{\text{NbC}}$ between the precipitate and matrix. Diffraction spots are indexed with red labels (0$\overline{2}$2, 002, 0$\overline{2}$0) and yellow labels (0$\overline{1}$1, 0$\overline{1}$1), confirming the coexistence of austenite and NbC phases. This characterization identifies the nano-scale NbC precipitation responsible for grain refinement and strengthening mechanisms in the 0.02Nb steel variant.
